marketState: neutral-to-bullish, weather-driven upside capped by a storage surplus
summary: All priority datasets are populated, so this read has reasonable confidence. Weather is the standout: near-term demand is +15.2% vs 5y and the 8-16 day outlook is +30.1% vs 5y and above the historical max range, both pointing to an unusually hot stretch feeding cooling demand. Seasonality mildly agrees (price +4.2pp above its typical seasonal path, 63% odds of being higher in 4 weeks, Sep is historically the strongest month), but reliability is flagged Low (51pp spread) so this edge is soft. Front price is trading SLIGHTLY OVER fair value at every horizon (now, +7D, +14D, +21D), though the fair-value estimate itself climbs from $2.929 to $3.089 over the next 3 weeks, meaning the market is pricing in some of the same seasonal strength. The counterweight is fundamental: storage is +175 Bcf above its 5y average with a bearish +13 Bcf build surprise, and managed money is net short at just the 31st percentile of its 5-year range, consistent with funds leaning on that supply cushion.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk, take partial at TP1
longTerm.rationale: 8-16 day cooling demand is +30% vs 5y and above the max historical range, seasonality gives 63% odds of higher prices in 4 weeks with Sep the strongest month, and fair value itself is drifting up from $2.929 to $3.089 into +21D — all supportive of a seasonal grind higher. Tempered to medium (not high) conviction by the +175 Bcf storage surplus, a bearish storage surprise, and managed money sitting net short at the 31st percentile.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D demand forecast reverts to/below 5y normal, storage builds keep beating forecast and the surplus widens further, or price closes back below the ~$2.93 current fair-value estimate.

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk given price is already rich vs fair value
shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D demand is +15.2% vs 5y near the top of its range, and 15m/1h/4h technicals are bullish with RSI in the mid-50s (not overbought). Kept low conviction because front is already SLIGHTLY OVER fair value at every near horizon (e.g. +$0.346 now vs a band top of $3.429) and the Aug-Oct curve is in slight backwardation, both limiting near-term upside room.
shortTerm.invalidation: RSI/MACD roll over across the 1h-4h timeframes, price breaks below $3.15, or the 1-7D demand outlook fades back toward normal.

keyDrivers: Extreme cooling demand: 8-16D forecast +30.1% vs 5y and above max range; 1-7D also +15.2% vs 5y
Seasonality tilts bullish: 63% odds higher in 4wks, Sep historically the strongest month, but reliability flagged Low
Fair value is rising over time ($2.929 now to $3.089 by +21D) even though front trades slightly above each estimate
Storage surplus (+175 Bcf vs 5y, bearish +13 Bcf surprise) is the main bearish counterweight
Managed money net short at the 31st percentile of 5y, consistent with the storage-driven bearish fundamental backdrop
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low (51pp 10-90% spread), so the seasonal edge could easily fail to play out
Daily/weekly technicals show a death cross (EMA50<200), raising risk that a longer-term downtrend reasserts
Storage surplus could keep widening if injections keep beating forecasts, capping any weather-driven rally
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
